contentBody: Which statement best describes NumPy ndarray?
option.1: It can store mixed Python objects of different types for speed.
option.2: It is a homogeneous contiguous-memory array that supports vectorized operations.
option.3: It is only for plotting charts and graphs.
option.4: It is slower than Python lists for numerical math because it avoids C code.
answerExplanation: ndarray is homogeneous and stored contiguously which enables vectorized high-performance numerical computation.

contentBody: Which command correctly creates a 2x3 array of zeros?
answerExplanation: np.zeros expects a shape tuple for multidimensional arrays.

contentBody: Select all correct statements about basic NumPy attributes.
option.2: arr.ndim is the number of axes.
option.3: arr.dtype() is the correct way to access dtype.
answerExplanation: shape ndim and size are correct attribute uses while dtype should be accessed as arr.dtype without parentheses.

contentBody: For a = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4]), what is the shape of a.reshape(-1, 1)?
answerExplanation: reshape(-1 1) creates a column vector with 4 rows and 1 column.

contentBody: Choose all true statements comparing np.arange and np.linspace.
option.2: np.linspace always excludes the stop value.
option.3: np.linspace is preferred when exact number of points is required.
option.4: Float-step np.arange can be sensitive to floating-point rounding.
answerExplanation: arange excludes stop and can have floating rounding issues while linspace is better when exact point count is needed.

contentBody: Given arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]); s = arr[1:4]; s[0] = 99, what is arr afterward?
answerExplanation: NumPy slices are views so modifying the slice modifies the original array.

contentBody: Given g1 and g2 each of shape (2, 2), select all operations that produce shape (2, 4).
answerExplanation: Horizontal concatenation and hstack add columns to produce shape (2 4); vstack and stack give different shapes.

contentBody: Why is NumPy usually much faster than Python lists for large numeric element-wise operations?
option.1: NumPy uses mixed data types in one array to reduce conversions.
option.2: NumPy operations are executed as vectorized compiled routines over contiguous memory.
option.3: NumPy always runs operations in parallel on GPU.
option.4: Python lists store raw integers without object overhead.
answerExplanation: NumPy speed mainly comes from contiguous memory layout and compiled vectorized kernels that avoid Python-loop overhead.
